prompt_text:
Analyze the following java source code repository for violations of the Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP).

## Principle Definition
High-level modules should not depend on low-level modules; both should depend on abstractions. Abstractions should not depend on details; details should depend on abstractions. When a class directly instantiates its dependencies instead of receiving them through injection, it violates DIP.

## What to Look For
- Classes that directly instantiate their dependencie...

  "findings": [

      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/JsonGenerator.java",
      "entity_name": "JsonGenerator.streamWriteConstraints",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 57,
      "line_end": 59,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Abstraction depends on a concrete implementation of constraints.",
      "reasoning": "The JsonGenerator abstract class (an abstraction) depends on the concrete StreamWriteConstraints class. DIP states that abstractions should not depend on details; both should depend on abstractions. By returning a concrete class and hard-coding a call to StreamWriteConstraints.defaults(), the base API is coupled to a specific implementation of constraint logic."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/JsonGenerator.java",
      "entity_name": "JsonGenerator.writeBinary",
      "entity_type": "method",

      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "High-level abstraction depends on concrete Base64Variants utility.",
      "reasoning": "The JsonGenerator abstraction depends on the concrete Base64Variants class to obtain a default encoding variant. This creates a hard-coded dependency between the high-level generator API and a specific set of low-level implementation details (Base64 variants), making it difficult to swap or mock the default encoding behavior."

      "description": "Parser implementation depends on concrete IOContext for buffer management.",
      "reasoning": "The parser constructor receives and depends on a concrete IOContext class. This dependency is passed up to the JsonParserBase abstraction. IOContext is a low-level module responsible for buffer allocation and lifecycle management.
